tasks.prompt: After a custom Collector dependency upgrade, an anonymously embedded helper gained a confmap decoder. Identify the promoted decoder owner, affected sibling, and Go source location before merge.
A named helper flattened with a squash tag rejects two parent options after a Collector library update. Locate the decoder responsible and list the sibling fields at risk.
A decoder is promoted through two levels of embedded configuration and a parent option becomes invalid. Trace the final method owner and report the source location.
A custom Collector config embeds a type alias whose decoder may receive the whole parent map. Determine the declared decoder owner and impacted sibling before upgrading.
A generic embedded helper implements confmap decoding and a peer field may be skipped or rejected. Produce a machine-readable source-level preflight result.
An embedded helper exposes its custom decoder on a value receiver. Check whether the method is promoted onto the parent and name the sibling field involved.
Check whether a built Collector configuration containing an unexpected receiver key is rejected before startup; Go source ownership is not required.
Run the standard general Go correctness and bug checks on a package; no Collector-specific decoder diagnosis is requested.
Generate JSON Schema from exported Go configuration structs for a component migration; runtime decoding diagnosis is not requested.
Automatically rewrite every embedded Collector configuration into nested YAML and commit the changes without review.
Prove that an explicit parent decoder preserves every sibling field without executing or inspecting its semantic tests.
Validate arbitrary YAML syntax, resolve credentials, and confirm that every telemetry backend is reachable.
